title: Fine-tuning, LoRA, and RLHF
summary: How to adapt pre-trained LLMs: full fine-tuning, parameter-efficient methods, and alignment via RLHF.
key_concepts: Full fine-tuning: update all params — expensive, catastrophic forgetting risk
LoRA: W = W_0 + AB where rank(AB) << rank(W) — train only A,B
QLoRA: quantize base model to 4-bit, LoRA adapters in 16-bit
Instruction tuning: train on (instruction, response) pairs
DPO: direct preference optimization — simpler alternative to RLHF
exercises: Fine-tune a small LLM (GPT-2) with LoRA using HuggingFace PEFT.
What is rank in LoRA and how do you choose it?
Explain RLHF pipeline: what is the reward model trained on?
Why does DPO not require a separate reward model?
code_example:
from peft import LoraConfig, get_peft_model
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM

model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained("gpt2")

lora_config = LoraConfig(
    r=16,           # rank
    lora_alpha=32,  # scaling factor
    target_modules=["c_attn", "c_proj"],  # which layers to adapt
    lora_dropout=0.1,
    bias="none"
)

peft_model = get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
peft_model.print_trainable_parameters()
# trainable params: 294,912 || all params: 124,734,720 || trainable%: 0.24%
thinking_questions: Why is LoRA so parameter-efficient? What's the intuition behind low-rank updates?
What is catastrophic forgetting and how does LoRA mitigate it?
